notes:
Ember 1.10.0 — Connect-from-anywhere finally works, plus a deep bug-fix pass

• Ember Link "Connect from anywhere" now actually works: the private link signs your phone in automatically from any network (it previously could never authenticate off Wi-Fi). Tapping the mirror now clicks the exact right spot even when the image is letterboxed, taps are sent atomically (no more occasional wrong-place clicks), quick-action buttons show the REAL result instead of always a checkmark, and the live view can no longer freeze on a dropped frame.
• Security: fixed a key-vault bug that could wipe all your saved API keys if the vault file was damaged (it now backs up and refuses instead of overwriting); an antivirus scan that couldn't read a file is no longer reported as "clean"; and the security monitor stopped falsely flagging local dev tools (Jupyter, dev servers) as backdoors.
• Voice: the free neural voice no longer goes silently mute on Windows/Linux (it played MP3 through a WAV-only player); it now falls back cleanly if playback can't happen.
• "Sleep" now really sleeps instead of hibernating; calendar events created from a specific timezone no longer land an hour off; the emergency lockdown now cuts wired connections too, not just Wi-Fi; and a microphone-leak on a failed voice start was fixed.

Builds on 1.9.7 (which itself finally shipped everything from 1.9.2-1.9.6).
